name: Hidden Hills
seoTitle: Hidden Hills Landscape Design & Build | Living Colors
metaDescription: Estate landscape design for Hidden Hills' gated, equestrian properties. Fire-wise, private, HOA-approved grounds. Start your free guided quote in ~90 seconds.
h1: Landscape Design & Build for Hidden Hills Estates
heroSubhead: Grounds designed for the privacy, acreage, and equestrian character that make this gated community singular.
intro: Behind the gates of Hidden Hills, life moves at the pace of bridle trails and wide, ranch-style lots. Properties here are measured in acres, not feet, which changes everything about how a landscape should read. We design grounds that honor the community's rural spirit — split-rail fencing, generous lawns, mature shade trees — while delivering the polish and durability an estate of this caliber deserves.

The community's architectural review process is thorough, and we treat it as a partner rather than a hurdle. From equestrian-friendly footing and pasture edges to entry motor courts that suit a horse property, our team builds landscapes that satisfy the HOA's standards and your family's daily rhythm. Everything is planned for the long game: healthy soil, resilient plantings, and irrigation that respects the semi-arid climate.

localContext.title: Equestrian-Ready Grounds
localContext.body: Hidden Hills is horse country by design, with trails threading between properties. We plan durable footing, safe fencing lines, dust-controlled arenas, and pasture transitions that keep animals comfortable while preserving the manicured look homeowners expect near the residence.

localContext.title: Architectural Review Fluency
localContext.body: The community's review board scrutinizes fencing, setbacks, and streetscape character. We prepare thorough submittals and design to the guidelines from day one, so approvals move smoothly and your project timeline stays intact without costly redesigns midstream.

localContext.title: Fire-Wise Estate Buffers
localContext.body: Perched against open hillsides, Hidden Hills lots demand defensible space. We layer irrigated zones, low-fuel plantings, and thoughtful spacing near structures to reduce risk while keeping the grounds lush, private, and true to the community's estate aesthetic.

faqs.q: Do you work within Hidden Hills' architectural review requirements?
faqs.a: Yes. We regularly design to gated-community standards and prepare the documentation review boards ask for, including fencing details, setbacks, and streetscape considerations. Handling the guidelines early keeps your approval on schedule and avoids the redesigns that surprise homeowners who skip that step.

faqs.q: Can you design around an existing equestrian setup?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Many Hidden Hills properties balance stables, arenas, and pasture with refined living areas near the home. We integrate durable footing, safe fencing, and dust control while keeping the grounds elegant, so the horse facilities and the entertaining spaces feel like one cohesive estate.

faqs.q: How do you approach fire safety on hillside lots?
faqs.a: We build defensible space into the design rather than bolting it on later. That means low-fuel plantings and smart spacing near structures, irrigated green zones as buffers, and material choices that reduce risk. The result protects the home while staying private, lush, and consistent with the neighborhood.

faqs.q: How does the free guided quote work?
faqs.a: Our Smart Quote Wizard walks you through a short, guided intake — about 90 seconds — covering your property, goals, and priorities. It gives us the context to prepare a thoughtful, tailored response for your Hidden Hills estate. There's no cost and no obligation to begin the conversation.

name: Thousand Oaks
seoTitle: Thousand Oaks Landscape Design & Build | Living Colors
metaDescription: Oak-conscious landscape design for Thousand Oaks homes and estates. Fire-wise, water-smart, HOA-savvy. Get your free guided quote in about 90 seconds.
h1: Thoughtful Landscape Design & Build in Thousand Oaks
heroSubhead: Grounds shaped for the valley oaks, rolling terrain, and open-space edges that define this Conejo Valley city.
intro: Thousand Oaks earned its name honestly — heritage coast live oaks anchor neighborhoods from Wildwood to Lynn Ranch, and the city protects them fiercely. We design landscapes that celebrate those trees, working within protected root zones and canopy setbacks so the oaks thrive while your grounds gain structure, color, and comfortable places to gather beneath the shade.

The Conejo Valley's terrain runs from flat suburban tracts to hillside lots that back onto open space and the Santa Monica Mountains. That variety shapes our approach: water-wise plant palettes suited to the warm, dry summers, drainage that handles winter runoff on slopes, and defensible-space planning where homes meet the wildland. The goal is a landscape that feels rooted in this place, not imported from somewhere greener.

localContext.title: Heritage Oak Protection
localContext.body: The city's oak tree ordinance safeguards native coast live and valley oaks, restricting work within protected zones. We design around root systems and drip lines, choosing compatible plantings and permeable surfaces so mature trees stay healthy and your project clears review without conflict.

localContext.title: Wildland-Edge Defensible Space
localContext.body: Many Thousand Oaks homes border open space and mountain terrain where fire risk is real. We create layered defensible zones — low-fuel plantings, careful spacing, and irrigated buffers — that meet county expectations while keeping the landscape green, private, and visually cohesive.

localContext.title: Water-Smart Conejo Palettes
localContext.body: Summers here are warm and dry, and water efficiency matters. We favor climate-adapted and California-native plantings paired with high-efficiency irrigation and smart controllers, delivering lasting color and texture that shrugs off drought conditions and keeps utility costs sensible year-round.

faqs.q: Can you landscape around a protected oak on my property?
faqs.a: Yes, and we do it constantly in Thousand Oaks. We respect the tree's protected root zone and drip line, select oak-compatible understory plants, and use permeable materials that let the soil breathe. That approach keeps heritage oaks healthy and keeps your project aligned with the city's tree ordinance.

faqs.q: Do you handle defensible space for hillside homes?
faqs.a: We do. Properties bordering open space or the Santa Monica Mountains need real fire planning, so we design layered zones with low-fuel plantings, thoughtful spacing, and irrigated buffers near structures. It satisfies fire-safety expectations while keeping your grounds lush, private, and cohesive rather than looking cleared.

faqs.q: How do you keep water use reasonable in this climate?
faqs.a: Our designs lean on climate-adapted and native plantings, efficient drip irrigation, and weather-based smart controllers that water only when needed. In some areas we blend in artificial turf for high-use spaces. You get durable color and greenery through the dry Conejo summers without runaway water bills.

faqs.q: What does the free guided quote include?
faqs.a: The Smart Quote Wizard is a short, guided intake — roughly 90 seconds — where you share your property details, goals, and priorities. It helps us understand your Thousand Oaks home before we respond with tailored ideas and next steps. It's completely free, with no obligation to proceed.

name: Westlake Village
seoTitle: Westlake Village Landscape Design & Build | Living Colors
metaDescription: Resort-style landscape design for Westlake Village's lakeside and gated homes. HOA-fluent, water-smart, elegant. Free guided quote in about 90 seconds.
h1: Refined Landscape Design & Build in Westlake Village
heroSubhead: Grounds tuned to the lake, the gated enclaves, and the polished resort character of this master-planned community.
intro: Westlake Village was planned around its lake, and that resort sensibility carries into the neighborhoods that ring it. Whether your home sits in a guard-gated community like North Ranch or along the water in the Westlake Island enclaves, expectations run high for grounds that feel curated and serene. We design landscapes that match — clean lines, layered planting, and outdoor rooms built for California's indoor-outdoor lifestyle.

Nearly every property here answers to an HOA and its design guidelines, so we build that fluency into the process. We balance the community's cohesive, manicured aesthetic with the individuality homeowners want, all while keeping water use smart in a climate of hot summers and modest rainfall. The outcome is a landscape that elevates your home and slips seamlessly into the neighborhood's elevated standard.

localContext.title: HOA & Design-Guideline Fluency
localContext.body: Most Westlake Village homes sit within associations that govern plant palettes, hardscape, and street-facing character. We design to those guidelines and prepare clean submittals, so your grounds express personality while earning smooth approval and fitting the community's cohesive, manicured look.

localContext.title: Lakeside & Resort-Style Living
localContext.body: The lake sets the tone for the whole city. For waterfront and view properties we craft outdoor living areas, refined plantings, and sightline-conscious layouts that make the most of the setting, echoing the relaxed, resort-quality atmosphere Westlake Village is known for.

localContext.title: Water-Wise Without Compromise
localContext.body: Hot, dry summers reward efficient design. We combine climate-adapted plantings, high-efficiency irrigation, and selective artificial turf to hold color and lushness through the season, meeting regional water expectations while keeping the grounds looking effortlessly green and upscale.

faqs.q: Are you familiar with Westlake Village HOA design guidelines?
faqs.a: Very. Most properties here fall under associations that regulate planting, hardscape, and street presentation. We design to those standards and prepare the documentation boards expect, so your grounds still feel personal and distinctive while moving through approval smoothly and blending into the community's cohesive look.

faqs.q: Can you design a waterfront or view property?
faqs.a: Yes. Lakeside and view homes are among our favorite projects. We plan outdoor living spaces, refined plantings, and layouts that protect key sightlines, so the setting stays the star. The aim is a relaxed, resort-quality feel that suits both entertaining and quiet evenings by the water.

faqs.q: How do you keep a lush look while saving water?
faqs.a: We pair climate-adapted plantings with efficient drip irrigation and smart controllers, and we use artificial turf selectively where lawns get heavy use. That combination holds rich color and greenery through Westlake's hot, dry summers while respecting regional water expectations and keeping ongoing costs in check.

faqs.q: How do I get started with a quote?
faqs.a: Use our Smart Quote Wizard, a short guided intake that takes about 90 seconds. You'll share your property, goals, and priorities, and we'll follow up with tailored ideas for your Westlake Village home. It's free and carries no obligation, so it's an easy way to begin.

name: Pasadena
seoTitle: Pasadena Landscape Design & Build | Living Colors
metaDescription: Landscape design for Pasadena's Craftsman homes and historic estates. Period-sensitive, oak-aware, water-smart. Free guided quote in about 90 seconds.
h1: Landscape Design & Build for Pasadena Homes & Estates
heroSubhead: Grounds that honor the city's Craftsman legacy, historic districts, and mature tree canopy with a designer's restraint.
intro: Pasadena wears its history in its gardens. From the Arts & Crafts masterpieces of Prospect Park to the grand estates along Orange Grove Boulevard, this is a city where architecture and landscape were meant to speak the same language. We design grounds that respect that dialogue — deep porches met by layered plantings, stone and brick that echo original detailing, and a plant palette rooted in the region's Mediterranean climate.

Much of the city falls within historic districts and Landmark designations, where exterior changes invite careful review. We navigate those guidelines with genuine appreciation, preserving the character that makes neighborhoods like Bungalow Heaven and Madison Heights special. Add Pasadena's mature street trees and heritage oaks, its summer heat, and its water-conscious ethos, and the work becomes a balance of preservation, artistry, and practical resilience.

localContext.title: Historic-District Sensitivity
localContext.body: Neighborhoods like Bungalow Heaven and Madison Heights carry Landmark District protections that shape exterior and streetscape changes. We design in period-appropriate materials and forms, working within the guidelines so your landscape enhances the home's historic character rather than competing with it.

localContext.title: Craftsman & Period Garden Design
localContext.body: Pasadena's Arts & Crafts homes call for landscapes with matching integrity — natural stone, clinker brick, layered foundation plantings, and restrained color. We compose gardens that feel original to the house, extending its architecture outward instead of applying a generic contemporary template.

localContext.title: Heritage Trees & Summer Heat
localContext.body: Mature oaks, deodars, and street canopy define Pasadena's shade, and city ordinances protect significant trees. We design around root zones and canopy, choosing heat-tolerant, water-wise plantings that thrive in the valley's hot, dry summers while preserving the trees that give the city its grace.

faqs.q: Do you design within Pasadena's historic districts?
faqs.a: Yes. We regularly work in Landmark Districts like Bungalow Heaven and Madison Heights, where exterior and streetscape changes face review. We choose period-appropriate materials and forms and design to the guidelines, so your landscape strengthens the home's historic character while moving through the approval process cleanly.

faqs.q: Can you create a garden that suits a Craftsman bungalow?
faqs.a: That's a specialty. Arts & Crafts homes deserve landscapes with the same integrity — natural stone, clinker brick, layered foundation plantings, and a restrained palette. We extend the architecture into the garden so it feels original to the house, not like a modern design dropped onto a historic property.

faqs.q: How do you protect mature oaks and heritage trees?
faqs.a: Pasadena protects significant trees, and we treat them as assets. We design around root zones and canopy, avoid grade changes that stress the tree, and select compatible, water-wise understory plantings. That keeps deodars, oaks, and street canopy healthy while your new landscape settles in gracefully beneath them.

faqs.q: What happens when I request a quote?
faqs.a: Our Smart Quote Wizard guides you through a brief intake — about 90 seconds — covering your property, style, and goals. It gives us the context to respond thoughtfully about your Pasadena home, historic considerations included. The guided quote is free and comes with no obligation, so it's a simple first step.

name: San Marino
seoTitle: San Marino Landscape Design & Build | Living Colors
metaDescription: Estate landscape design for San Marino's historic homes and heritage oaks. Elegant, preservation-minded, water-smart. Free guided quote in about 90 seconds.
h1: Estate Landscape Design & Build in San Marino
heroSubhead: Grounds worthy of the city's stately homes, generous lots, and celebrated heritage tree canopy.
intro: Few communities in Southern California take their landscapes as seriously as San Marino. This is the city of the Huntington gardens, of broad boulevards lined with mature trees and gracious estates set well back from the street. Homes here — Mediterranean revivals, stately traditionals, refined ranches — deserve grounds composed with equal care. We design landscapes of quiet grandeur, where structure, symmetry, and mature planting carry the day.

San Marino's identity is inseparable from its trees, and the city guards them closely through a well-known tree preservation ordinance. Heritage oaks and towering specimens shape nearly every property, and we design in deference to them. Combine that with generous lot sizes, high aesthetic expectations, and a Mediterranean climate of hot summers and mild winters, and the work rewards patience, craftsmanship, and a deep respect for what's already rooted in the ground.

localContext.title: Heritage Oak & Tree Preservation
localContext.body: San Marino's tree ordinance is among the region's most protective, covering oaks and other significant specimens. We design around root systems and canopy, avoid harmful grade changes, and choose compatible plantings, so century-old trees stay healthy while your estate grounds gain refinement beneath them.

localContext.title: Estate-Scale Composition
localContext.body: Large, deep lots set behind mature frontage define San Marino. We compose at that scale — motor courts, symmetrical entries, layered gardens, and generous outdoor rooms — creating grounds with the sense of arrival and quiet grandeur these stately homes have always called for.

localContext.title: Period-Sensitive Elegance
localContext.body: The city's Mediterranean revivals and traditionals reward classic, restrained design. We favor timeless materials, considered symmetry, and a plant palette suited to the hot-summer Mediterranean climate, producing landscapes that feel established and enduring rather than trend-driven or transient.

faqs.q: How do you work with San Marino's tree preservation ordinance?
faqs.a: We treat it as central to the design. San Marino protects oaks and other significant trees closely, so we plan around root zones and canopy, avoid grade changes that stress them, and select compatible understory plantings. That keeps heritage trees thriving while your grounds gain the refinement the property deserves.

faqs.q: Do you design for large estate lots?
faqs.a: Yes, and San Marino's generous, deep lots are ideal for it. We compose at estate scale — motor courts, symmetrical entries, layered gardens, and expansive outdoor living areas — so the grounds create a genuine sense of arrival and quiet grandeur that suits these stately homes and their setbacks.

faqs.q: Can you match a landscape to a Mediterranean revival home?
faqs.a: Certainly. Period-sensitive design is core to how we work. For San Marino's revivals and traditionals we favor timeless materials, considered symmetry, and a climate-appropriate palette, so the landscape feels original and established rather than trendy. The goal is grounds that will look right for decades, not just this season.

faqs.q: How does the free guided quote work?
faqs.a: Our Smart Quote Wizard leads you through a short intake — roughly 90 seconds — where you share your property, style, and goals. It gives us what we need to respond thoughtfully about your San Marino estate, heritage trees and all. The guided quote is free and carries no obligation to move forward.
